tasks.title: Spike: MercadoPago preapproval amount-mutation capability
tasks.description:
Investigate (against current MP docs via Context7 / MP API reference) whether a live preapproval's `transaction_amount` can be mutated reliably for discounted cycles and restored after:
- Which PATCH/PUT endpoint mutates amount on a live preapproval (does `/v1/preapproval/{id}` accept `transaction_amount`?).
- Timing window: can the amount be changed after a cycle starts? Before it? Is there a minimum lead-time?
- Webhook implications: does a mutation trigger a new webhook? Does it shift the next-charge date?
- Idempotency: is the PATCH idempotent? What happens on a retry?
- Outcome A (MP CAN mutate reliably): document the exact endpoint + field + timing constraint + idempotency strategy. Case-3 tasks (T-007) proceed with amount-mutation as the mechanism.
- Outcome B (MP CANNOT mutate reliably): write a clear finding doc; mark T-007 as BLOCKED and surface redesign requirement to owner before proceeding. Do NOT fall back to refunds/credits silently (VETOED in OQ-4).

Deliverable: `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/promo-code/docs/mp-preapproval-mutation-spike.md` with findings, chosen mechanism (A or B), and any timing/idempotency constraints. This doc closes OQ-4 concretely.

Acceptance criteria: the doc exists, answers all bullet questions above, states a go/no-go for Outcome A, and is referenced from T-007's description before T-007 starts.

tasks.title: Add typed effect columns to @repo/db promo-code + subscription schemas
tasks.description:
Structural DB changes (via `pnpm db:generate` + `pnpm db:migrate` — structural carril):

`billing_promo_codes` — add explicit typed columns (OQ-2 decision):
- `effect_kind varchar(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'discount'` — values: 'discount' | 'trial_extension' | 'comp'
- `value_kind varchar(20)` — values: 'percentage' | 'fixed'; NULL for non-discount effects
- `value integer` — discount value (centavos for fixed, integer 0-100 for percentage); NULL for non-discount
- `duration_cycles integer` — NULL = forever; positive integer for N cycles; NULL for trial_extension/comp
- `extra_days integer` — NULL for non-trial-extension effects

`billing_subscriptions` — add:
- `promo_effect_remaining_cycles integer` — countdown for multi-cycle discounts; NULL when no active discount or discount is forever

Drizzle model files affected:
- `packages/db/src/schema/billing/billing-promo-codes.schema.ts`
- `packages/db/src/schema/billing/billing-subscriptions.schema.ts`

Generate migration with `pnpm db:generate`. Commit the generated migration file.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC-1.1 (effect fields readable via API after persist)
- AC-2.4 (comp state explicit in DB)
- `pnpm db:generate` produces no additional drift after this task
- `pnpm typecheck` passes

tasks.title: Add DB CHECK constraints + backfill SQL (extras carril 018)
tasks.description:
Hand-written idempotent SQL for `packages/db/src/migrations/extras/018-promo-code-effect-constraints.sql` (extras carril — re-applied by `pnpm db:apply-extras`):

CHECK constraints on `billing_promo_codes`:
- When `effect_kind = 'discount'`: `value_kind` NOT NULL, `value` NOT NULL, `value >= 0`, percentage `value <= 100`
- When `effect_kind = 'trial_extension'`: `extra_days` NOT NULL, `extra_days > 0`, `value` IS NULL, `value_kind` IS NULL
- When `effect_kind = 'comp'`: `value` IS NULL, `value_kind` IS NULL, `extra_days` IS NULL, `duration_cycles` IS NULL
- `duration_cycles > 0` when NOT NULL

Data backfill (idempotent, dry-run count step first):
- All existing rows where `effect_kind` IS NULL → set `effect_kind = 'discount'`, `value_kind` = existing `type` (mapped 'percentage'→'percentage', 'fixed'→'fixed'), `value` from existing discount value col, `duration_cycles = 1` (one-shot, AC-4.1)
- `HOSPEDA_FREE` (code = 'HOSPEDA_FREE') → `effect_kind = 'comp'` (OQ-1 β; fixes AC-2.3 latent bug)
- `FREEMONTH` → `effect_kind = 'trial_extension'`, `extra_days = 30`
- `LANZAMIENTO50` → `effect_kind = 'discount'`, `value_kind = 'percentage'`, `value = 50`, `duration_cycles = 3`
- `BIENVENIDO30` → `effect_kind = 'discount'`, `value_kind = 'percentage'`, `value = 30`, `duration_cycles = 1`

Also: subscription reconciliation — any `billing_subscriptions` row with `promo_code_id` pointing to HOSPEDA_FREE → set `status = 'comp'` if currently `active` (comp supersedes active billing).

SQL must be idempotent (use `DO $$ IF NOT EXISTS ... $$` or `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` pattern; re-running produces zero rows changed).

Acceptance criteria:
- AC-2.3 (HOSPEDA_FREE migrated, no longer a latent bug)
- AC-4.1 (legacy codes preserved as duration_cycles=1)
- CHECK constraints reject impossible combos at DB level (AC-1.2, AC-1.3 enforcement layer)
- `pnpm db:apply-extras` succeeds idempotently (second run = 0 rows changed)

tasks.title: Extend @repo/schemas promo-code Zod schemas for typed effects
tasks.description:
Extend schemas in `packages/schemas/src/api/billing/promo-code.schema.ts`:

- Add `PromoEffectKindEnum` and `ValueKindEnum` enums
- Replace the current `discountType: 'percentage' | 'fixed'` flat field with a discriminated union `effect` discriminated on `kind`:
  - `discount`: `{ kind: 'discount', valueKind: 'percentage' | 'fixed', value: number, durationCycles: number | null }`
    - refines: percentage value ≤ 100 (AC-1.2), durationCycles > 0 when not null (AC-1.3)
  - `trial_extension`: `{ kind: 'trial_extension', extraDays: number }` (positive integer)
  - `comp`: `{ kind: 'comp' }` (no extra params)
- Update `CreatePromoCodeSchema` (admin create/edit input) and `PromoCodeResponseSchema` (API response / admin form hydration)
- Keep backward-compat: existing one-shot codes serialize as `{ kind: 'discount', valueKind: ..., value: ..., durationCycles: 1 }`
- Export inferred `CreatePromoCodeInput`, `PromoCodeResponse`, `PromoEffect` types
- Unit tests: valid combos parse, invalid (percentage > 100, negative days, durationCycles = 0, comp with value) are rejected

Acceptance criteria:
- AC-1.2, AC-1.3, AC-5.1–AC-5.4 (schema is the single source of truth for admin form validation)
- AC-4.3 (one-shot response shape unchanged for clients using durationCycles=1)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes across all packages

tasks.title: Extend service-core promo-code CRUD + effect-engine branching
tasks.description:
Changes to `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/promo-code/`:

1. `promo-code.crud.ts` — update `mapDbToPromoCode` to read the new typed columns and populate the `effect` discriminated union on the DTO. Legacy rows (effect_kind = 'discount', duration_cycles = 1) map identically to the current shape.

2. `promo-code.service.ts` (PromoCode facade) — expose `createPromoCode`/`updatePromoCode` accepting the new `CreatePromoCodeInput` discriminated union; persist `effect_kind`, `value_kind`, `value`, `duration_cycles`, `extra_days` to the DB columns.

3. `promo-code.redemption.ts::applyPromoCode` — branch by `effect.kind` instead of assuming money discount:
   - `discount`: existing `finalAmount` computation (unchanged for durationCycles=1, AC-4.3); set `promo_effect_remaining_cycles = duration_cycles - 1` on the subscription (NULL when durationCycles is null = forever)
   - `trial_extension` at checkout: translate to `freeTrialDays` for qzpay subscription-create (existing path, now reads from persisted DB effect, NOT from `@repo/billing` config)
   - `comp`: create subscription with `status = 'comp'`, no MP preapproval created

4. Retire / stub `resolveFreeTrialExtensionPromo()` in `@repo/billing` — it now reads from the DB-persisted effect via the promo-code service. Keep the function signature for now but delegate to the DB lookup.

Unit tests (AAA pattern, real DB fixtures or seeded test data):
- discount durationCycles=1 → finalAmount identical to current (AC-4.1 regression lock)
- discount durationCycles=3 → remaining_cycles set to 2 after first cycle
- comp → status='comp', no MP preapproval param emitted
- trial_extension at checkout → freeTrialDays populated correctly

Acceptance criteria: AC-1.4, AC-2.1, AC-3.2, AC-4.1, AC-4.3

tasks.title: Implement trial-extension-on-existing-subscription service operation
tasks.description:
New operation in `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/promo-code/promo-code.trial-extension.ts` (or extend `promo-code.service.ts`):

`extendExistingSubscriptionTrial({ subscriptionId, promoCodeId, actorId })` — RO-RO:
1. Load subscription; verify it is in `trialing` status (reject with typed `VALIDATION_ERROR` if not — AC-3.4)
2. Load promo code; verify `effect.kind === 'trial_extension'` (typed error otherwise)
3. If annual sub (`mp_subscription_id = null`) and NOT in trial → reject typed error (AC-3.5); if annual AND in trial → proceed to extend `trial_end` only
4. Push `billing_subscriptions.trial_end` by `effect.extraDays` days (arithmetic in UTC)
5. For monthly subs with live `mp_subscription_id`: call the MP preapproval adjustment (or note it as a TODO pointing to T-007 finding, since the exact mechanism is part of the spike domain). Persist the new `trial_end` regardless and emit a reconciliation flag for the webhook path.
6. Record usage in `billing_promo_code_usage`
7. Return typed result with new `trial_end` and any MP reconciliation status

Unit tests:
- trialing sub → trial_end pushed by extraDays, usage row created (AC-3.1)
- active (non-trialing) sub → VALIDATION_ERROR, no state change (AC-3.4)
- annual sub in trial → trial_end pushed (AC-3.5 affirm path)
- annual sub past trial → VALIDATION_ERROR (AC-3.5 reject path)
- non-trial-extension effect code → typed error

Ownership/permission guard is enforced at the API route layer (T-009); this service trusts the caller has verified authorization.

Acceptance criteria: AC-3.1, AC-3.4, AC-3.5

tasks.title: Implement multi-cycle discount renewal decrement (case 3, post-spike)
tasks.description:
**BLOCKED by T-001 (spike outcome must be Outcome A — MP can mutate amount).**
If spike finds Outcome B, STOP and surface redesign to owner before implementing anything in this task.

Based on Outcome A (spike doc confirms amount-mutation works):

1. `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/promo-code/promo-code.renewal.ts` (new file):
   `resolveRenewalPromoEffect({ subscriptionId })` — called by the webhook/poll path:
   a. Load subscription; check `promo_code_id` and `promo_effect_remaining_cycles`
   b. If `remaining_cycles IS NULL` and `effect.kind = 'discount'` and `duration_cycles IS NULL` → forever discount (apply indefinitely)
   c. If `remaining_cycles > 0` → apply discount, decrement `remaining_cycles` by 1
   d. If `remaining_cycles = 0` → effect exhausted; full price; clear `promo_code_id` link (or mark exhausted)
   e. For comp subs (`status = 'comp'`): amount = 0; no preapproval interaction needed (OQ-1 β removes case 1 from this path)

2. MP preapproval amount mutation (per spike doc mechanism):
   - Before each discounted cycle: PATCH preapproval with discounted `transaction_amount`
   - After cycle confirmed paid (webhook): restore `transaction_amount` to base price for the next cycle (or only if remaining_cycles will hit 0)
   - Timing constraints from spike doc must be honored
   - If MP PATCH fails: emit Sentry error, do NOT silently charge full price without alerting

3. Wire into the webhook handler path (`apps/api/src/routes/billing/webhooks/` or the subscription-poll job) — call `resolveRenewalPromoEffect` before emitting the charge amount.

4. `dunning.job.ts` and `subscription-poll.job.ts` must NOT correct a comp/discounted subscription back to full price — add guards.

Unit tests:
- Cycle 1 (remaining=2): amount discounted, remaining decremented to 1 (AC-1.5 first cycle)
- Cycle 3 (remaining=0): amount full, effect exhausted (AC-1.5 stop cycle)
- Forever (remaining=NULL, duration_cycles=NULL): amount discounted, remaining stays NULL (AC-2.2)
- Comp: amount=0, no MP mutation called (AC-2.1, AC-2.2)
- Cron guard: dunning does not flag a comp/discounted cycle as delinquent (risk mitigations §13)

Acceptance criteria: AC-1.5, AC-2.1, AC-2.2, AC-3.3 (MP date adjustment), AC-4.2

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE (post-spike, event-driven per spike §5):** service-core `resolveRenewalPromoEffect` DECIDES (apply-discount | restore-full | comp | noop) + persists the decremented counter; apps/api EXECUTES the MP mutation. Webhook tree = `apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/` (NOT billing/webhooks); wired into `subscription-payment-handler.ts` `subscription_authorized_payment.{created,updated}` handler. Fail-closed initial discount apply (`applyInitialDiscountMutation`) + best-effort-with-retry restore (`restoreFullPriceMutation`) in `apps/api/src/services/promo-renewal-mp.service.ts`. Initial-apply seam for existing subs = `apps/api/src/services/promo-discount-apply.service.ts` (TODO(T-008/T-009) wiring). dunning guard added; subscription-poll is discount-aware by construction (only activates pending subs; documented).

tasks.title: Update admin API routes: promo-code create/edit + /apply endpoint
tasks.description:
Changes to `apps/api/src/routes/billing/promo-codes.ts` (and admin sub-router):

1. **Admin create/edit** (`POST /api/v1/admin/billing/promo-codes`, `PATCH .../billing/promo-codes/:id`):
   - Remove the hardcoded `discountType: 'percentage' | 'fixed'` input constraint
   - Accept `CreatePromoCodeInput` discriminated union from `@repo/schemas`
   - Validate via Zod (AC-1.2, AC-1.3, AC-5.4 — 422 on invalid effect params)
   - Persist via updated `promo-code.service.ts`
   - Permission guard: `BILLING_PROMO_CODE_MANAGE` (AC-6.1)

2. **Protected /apply** (`POST /api/v1/protected/billing/promo-codes/apply`):
   - Branch by `effect.kind` (read from DB via service) instead of assuming money discount
   - `discount`: existing response shape unchanged (`discountAmount`, `finalAmount` — AC-4.3)
   - `trial_extension` at checkout: return effect info (extraDays, new projected trial_end)
   - `comp`: return comp indication
   - Ownership guard: `customerId` must be own customer or `ACCESS_API_ADMIN` (AC-6.2)

Integration tests (real DB, mock MP):
- Admin create discount (success 201 + 422 bad value + 422 durationCycles=0 + 403 no permission)
- Admin create trial_extension (success + 422 negative days)
- Admin create comp (success)
- /apply discount → unchanged response shape (AC-4.3)
- /apply comp → comp indication returned
- /apply 403 (other customer's customerId)

Acceptance criteria: AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.3, AC-4.3, AC-5.4, AC-6.1, AC-6.2

tasks.title: Add API route: apply trial extension to existing subscription
tasks.description:
New route: `POST /api/v1/admin/billing/subscriptions/:subscriptionId/apply-trial-extension`

- Accept `{ promoCodeId: string }` body (validate with Zod)
- Auth: `BILLING_PROMO_CODE_MANAGE` permission required
- Delegate to `extendExistingSubscriptionTrial()` from T-006
- Map typed service errors to HTTP responses:
  - `VALIDATION_ERROR` (non-trial sub) → 422
  - `NOT_FOUND` → 404
  - Unrelated customer without admin permission → 403 (AC-6.2)
- On success: return updated subscription with new `trial_end`

Integration tests:
- trialing sub + valid code → 200 + updated trial_end (AC-3.1)
- active (non-trial) sub → 422 (AC-3.4)
- 403 without BILLING_PROMO_CODE_MANAGE
- non-trial-extension code → 422
- annual sub in trial → 200 (AC-3.5 accept)
- annual sub past trial → 422 (AC-3.5 reject)

Acceptance criteria: AC-3.1, AC-3.4, AC-3.5, AC-6.1

tasks.title: Admin UI: promo-code create/edit form with effect-type selector
tasks.description:
Update `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/billing/promo-codes/` (create and edit forms):

1. Effect-type selector (`<Select>`) bound to TanStack Form field `effect.kind`
2. Conditional parameter panels (Shadcn UI, Tailwind):
   - `discount`: valueKind radio + value input + durationCycles input with "forever" toggle (null)
   - `trial_extension`: extraDays input (positive integer; UX hint: "X months = X×30 days"); no value/durationCycles controls
   - `comp`: no extra params shown (AC-5.1–AC-5.3)
3. Client validation mirrors Zod schema (percentage ≤ 100, days > 0, durationCycles > 0 when set — AC-5.4)
4. Submit handler sends `CreatePromoCodeInput` discriminated union to API
5. i18n keys under `billing.promoCode.effect.*` namespace in `@repo/i18n` (es/en/pt)
6. On GET /promo-codes/:id (edit): hydrate form from `PromoCodeResponse.effect` (effect-type selector pre-selected)

Component tests (testing-library/react):
- Selecting `trial_extension` hides value/durationCycles, shows extraDays
- Selecting `comp` hides all param inputs
- percentage > 100 blocks form submit (client validation)
- negative extraDays blocks submit
- i18n keys resolve in es locale

Acceptance criteria: AC-5.1, AC-5.2, AC-5.3, AC-5.4, AC-6.1 (form guards admin permission server-side)

tasks.title: Update admin subscription detail view for active promo effect display
tasks.description:
Update `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/billing/subscriptions/` (subscription detail page) and the subscription list's badge/indicator:

1. Show active promo effect on subscription detail:
   - `discount` with remaining_cycles > 0: "Discount: -X% for N more cycles"
   - `discount` with remaining_cycles = NULL (forever): "Discount: -X% forever"
   - `comp`: "Complimentary (never billed)" (AC-2.4 — explicit, not confusable with discount)
   - `trial_extension`: show extended `trial_end` date + "Trial extended +N days"
   - Exhausted effect (remaining_cycles = 0): "Promo exhausted" (neutral, not an error state)
2. Subscription list: add a pill/badge for `comp` status so support can see at a glance
3. i18n keys under `billing.subscription.promoEffect.*` (es/en/pt)
4. UX: explicit messages for all edge cases (AC-6 UX notes §6): annual-sub rejection, already-exhausted code, expired code

Component tests:
- Renders "Complimentary" for comp subs (not a discount amount)
- Renders remaining-cycle count for active multi-cycle discount
- Renders trial_end extension date
- Exhausted effect renders neutral state

Acceptance criteria: AC-2.4, AC-6 UX considerations §6

tasks.title: Update checkout + apply feedback (web-facing copy for multi-cycle effects)
tasks.description:
Update the web-facing promo-code apply flow (wherever the `/apply` response is consumed in `apps/web/`):

1. When `effect.kind = 'discount'` with `durationCycles > 1`: show "50% off for 3 months" (NOT just a single-cycle amount)
2. When `effect.kind = 'discount'` with `durationCycles = null` (forever): show "Free forever" or "Permanent discount"
3. When `effect.kind = 'comp'`: show "Your account is complimentary — never billed"
4. When `effect.kind = 'trial_extension'`: show "Trial extended by N days (until <date>)"
5. When applying to an annual sub outside trial → surface the typed 422 error with a clear message
6. i18n keys under `billing.promoApply.*` in `@repo/i18n` (es/en/pt)

Note: web uses vanilla CSS / CSS Modules (no Tailwind classes in web). Forms use native HTML.

Unit/integration tests:
- Apply response with durationCycles=3 → copy shows "3 months" not just the discount amount
- Apply comp → shows complimentary copy
- 422 annual-past-trial → shows specific error message

Acceptance criteria: AC-5.1 UX (checkout feedback), §6 UX Considerations

tasks.title: Migration regression tests + HOSPEDA_FREE latent-bug regression lock
tasks.description:
Dedicated test file: `packages/service-core/src/services/billing/promo-code/test/promo-code.migration.test.ts`

1. **HOSPEDA_FREE latent-bug regression (AC-2.3):**
   - Seed a subscription linked to HOSPEDA_FREE code
   - Assert cycle-1 charge = 0
   - Assert cycle-2 charge = 0 (the bug: pre-migration this would return full price)
   - Assert `status = 'comp'` not a discount effect

2. **Backfill idempotency:**
   - Apply the 018 extras SQL twice against the test DB
   - Assert second run changes 0 rows

3. **Legacy code preservation (AC-4.1):**
   - Seed a legacy `billing_promo_codes` row with old `type = 'percentage'`, `discount_value = 30`
   - After backfill: assert `effect_kind = 'discount'`, `duration_cycles = 1`, same `value`
   - Assert `applyPromoCode` returns identical `finalAmount` as before migration

4. **Effect-param CHECK violations:**
   - Attempt to INSERT a row with percentage value = 150 → DB rejects (CHECK constraint)
   - Attempt comp with `extra_days` set → DB rejects
   - Attempt trial_extension with no `extra_days` → DB rejects

Acceptance criteria: AC-2.3 (cycle-2 regression lock), AC-4.1 (legacy codes), AC-1.2/AC-1.3 (DB-level enforcement)

tasks.title: SPEC-143 MercadoPago staging + prod smoke (billing CORE gate)
tasks.description:
MANDATORY per project rule (CLAUDE.md §Billing testing): any PR touching billing CORE (charge computation on recurring preapprovals, trial/preapproval adjustment) requires BOTH staging AND prod smoke sign-offs.

**Staging smoke** (against `https://staging.hospeda.com.ar` with real MP sandbox):
1. Multi-cycle discount (LANZAMIENTO50 / 50% × 3 cycles) across ≥ 2 real renewal cycles:
   - Cycle 1: charged at 50%, remaining_cycles decremented to 2
   - Cycle 2 (if time allows in sandbox): charged at 50%, remaining_cycles = 1
2. Free-forever comp subscription across ≥ 2 cycles:
   - status = 'comp', charge = 0 both cycles
3. Trial extension applied to a live preapproval (FREEMONTH):
   - trial_end pushed out, MP next-charge date reconciled (AC-3.3)
4. Legacy one-shot code (BIENVENIDO30) → same discount as pre-migration (AC-4.1 regression)

Use cards from `.qtm/specs/SPEC-143-billing-testing-coverage/docs/mp-test-cards-reference.md`.
File sign-off in `.qtm/specs/SPEC-143-billing-testing-coverage/docs/staging-smoke-checklist.md`.

**Prod smoke** (same flows after staging→main promotion):
File sign-off in `.qtm/specs/SPEC-143-billing-testing-coverage/docs/prod-smoke-checklist.md`.

Note: the vitest suite uses an MP stub and CANNOT substitute for this smoke. Failed smoke blocks merge.

Acceptance criteria: AC-1.5 (multi-cycle stops at N), AC-2.2 (free-forever ≥2 cycles), AC-3.3 (MP trial date), AC-4.1 (legacy regression), SPEC-143 sign-off filed.

tasks.title: Docs: update billing CLAUDE.md + promo-code engine architecture note
tasks.description:
Documentation updates (NO implementation code):

1. `packages/service-core/CLAUDE.md` (or `packages/billing/CLAUDE.md`): add a section describing the typed effect engine:
   - Effect kinds (discount / trial_extension / comp) and their parameters
   - Where each seam lives (`promo-code.redemption.ts`, `promo-code.trial-extension.ts`, `promo-code.renewal.ts`)
   - The `comp` subscription state and why it does NOT create an MP preapproval
   - How the renewal decrement works (remaining_cycles column) and when the effect is exhausted
   - Reference to the MP spike doc (`mp-preapproval-mutation-spike.md`)

2. `CLAUDE.md` (project root) billing quick reference: add a line about `billing_promo_codes.effect_kind` + `billing_subscriptions.promo_effect_remaining_cycles` + `comp` status, analogous to the `product_domain` gotcha entries.

3. If the spike (T-001) produced Outcome B: document the deferred-redesign decision clearly so a new session does not restart the spike.

Acceptance criteria: no AC number (docs only); docs are accurate, no stale references to config-only effect fields.
